Document:
# Novakin
## Overview
Novakin are an ethereal, sapient species from a higher dimension. Normally they are able to come and go as they please with noone the wiser, but for unknown reasons, some end up stuck in our own reality and cannot return to their own. In order to achieve form and thus independence from the fabric of space and time, they surround themselves with superheated gas which as a result of their other worldly origins, has to be kept at temperatures that are normally lethal for other organisms.
However, due to them not being adapted to this reality, their constintuent gas slowly dissipates over time if they are not inside an appropriate pressure bearing vessel, requiring them to not only wear pressure suits, but also replenish their gas periodically.
## Anatomy
A Novakin has nominally one organ: a Nexus that contains their consciousness, however they also have an extended 'psuedo organ' in the form of a core which circulates and stabilizes its gaseous form as well as processes chemicals.
Loss of the core prevents the Novakin from being able to maintain their structural integrity, thus causing their form and nexus to 'fade' into the fabric of reality. The destruction of the Nexus truly kills the Novakin.
Gasses a Novakin can take form with consist of Nitrogen, Oxygen, Carbon Dioxide, Water Vapor, Nitrous Oxide, and Ammonia. However, there has been tales of Novakin of Plasma, Tritium, and Frezon constituiton.
Due to their alien nature, they do not need to breathe, eat, drink, or sleep, but it comes at the cost of not exactly being able to exist without their suits and inhalers.
## Temperature
A Novakin's normal operating temperature is [bold]373.15 K (100 C)[/bold]. Their core naturally regulates toward this temperature while it has fuel. Without fuel, they cool at an accelerated rate.
Pressure-protective outerwear greatly slows reserve loss. It does not make a Novakin invulnerable: a depleted reserve weakens thermal regulation, and a Novakin that falls below their damage threshold begins taking Cellular damage. Novakin do not passively heal damage.
Novakin resist Heat damage while below [bold]700 K[/bold], but take Heat damage if their core temperature exceeds that limit. They resist 60 percent of Radiation damage; their other damage tolerances are otherwise unchanged.
## Vision
Novakin are capable of seeing in complete darkness by seemingly supernatural means. Although when met with rapid intense flashes of light that they do not produce, they can be severely blinded.
## Gas Reserve
A Novakin's structural gas reserve leaks fastest while full and pressurized, then more slowly as pressure falls. Below a critical reserve, or while the kin is medically critical, containment becomes unstable and leakage accelerates sharply. Their selected gas escapes into the surrounding atmosphere; pressure-protected outerwear reduces that leakage.
The gas reserve is shown by the battery icon in the kin's HUD. Other players cannot determine a Novakin's chosen gas by examining them; they must ask the Novakin, or use a health analyzer. A health analyzer reports both structural integrity and gas type.
## Essential Equipment
Character loadouts provide a pressure-protective EVA suit appropriate to a Novakin's job. Wearing it whenever practical protects their reserve. Wearing both a pressure-protective suit and a pressure-protective helmet also contains the kin's emitted light, leaving them dark until the seal is broken.
Matching-gas inhalers restore reserve. Starting survival kits contain an adaptive inhaler configured for the gas selected during character creation. Inhalers only replenish a Novakin with the matching gas and can be refilled from a pure native-gas tank or canister.
## Discharge Core
At [bold]650 K[/bold] or hotter, a Novakin can use [bold]Discharge Core[/bold] to vent half of their remaining reserve into the surrounding atmosphere and shed [bold]300 K[/bold]. It is an emergency cooling measure, not a source of healing.
